boundaries.title_guess: Front matter: address, dedication & van pick-up note

evidence.excerpt: 86 Westfield Rd, Edgbaston, Birmingham, B153 JG. [glued-in postcard of Chenonceau]
Camping in the van. May 1989.... .... October 1992  Van Picked up by Jim, Margaret, Helen, Simon, Thomas, William, Peter.
1 Malvern+ Farm 1989 / 2 Harlech / 4 Peak District ... (index page, places listed against page numbers)
102 Beauchamp House / 103 Arlingham ... 140 Coningsby. (index continuation)
May. First we spent a day having a picnic at Malvern, visiting a quilting exhibition. Then we went to Kate & Jamie's farm for one night
Half-term at Dinas Camp near Harlech. May
June 10th Malvern old boys' day. A very useful base here. Joined Robert Upex. Mrs. Hill at No1.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Page A_0004_R opens with the tail of the Dinas/Harlech trip ('carrier, Thomas carried the food ... glorious sunshine + a fabulous holiday'); the break falls at 'June 10th Malvern old boys' day', which starts a short separate visit.

evidence.excerpt: July 1st. Weekend in the Peak District. We all slept in the van at Hopping Farm.

boundaries.title_guess: Dinas Camp near Harlech (July, with family)

evidence.excerpt: July 14th Friday. Off to Dinas Camp near Harlech again, to join Tim, Margaret, Helen and David, Sally, Maia. We left before breakfast

boundaries.title_guess: Northern tour: Leeds, York, Hadrian's Wall, Settle, Burnley, Warburton & Arley Hall

evidence.excerpt: John's birthday. Saturday Aug 19th. Off to the north. Leeds - camped on the Sykes front drive.
Mr. Rourke's factory on the way home. ... After Burnley, we drove to Warburton in Cheshire.

boundaries.title_guess: Stay with Venetia & David at Little Somborne

evidence.excerpt: September 29th. We went to stay with Venetia. David was cutting the grass for his 40th birthday. ... Harvest Festival in little Somborne church.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Page A_0010_L opens with the tail of the northern trip ('buy two pictures which had hung in the Hall 200 years ago.' - Arley Hall); the break falls at 'September 29th. We went to stay with Venetia', starting a family visit to Little Somborne.

boundaries.title_guess: Stokesay Castle, Shropshire (last trip of the year)

evidence.excerpt: October 28th Saturday. We have set off for the last trip of the year - a night in Shropshire. Stokesay Castle.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Page A_0010_R opens with the tail of the Venetia/Little Somborne visit ('church. A hymn about the rain bow ... Robert Welch's workshop in Chipping Camden on the way home'); the break falls at 'October 28th Saturday. We have set off for the last trip of the year - a night in Shropshire. Stokesay Castle.'

boundaries.title_guess: 1990: Day out to Windmill & Church with Doris & Adelbert

evidence.excerpt: 1990  April 6th. Day out to Windmill + Church with Doris + Adelbert

boundaries.mixed_detail: Page A_0011_R opens with the tail of the Stokesay Castle trip ('Lord + Lady's solar chamber ... Arrived at Hamperley campsite ... The year finished'); the break falls at '1990 April 6th. Day out to Windmill', which begins the 1990 season with a day out.

boundaries.title_guess: Di & Ian, Windmill Hill, Avebury, Devizes, Lacock, Bradford & Bath

evidence.excerpt: April 20th. We set off + left John + Charles with Grandma + Grandpa. Then on to Di + Ian ... We stayed the night in their garden

boundaries.title_guess: Hamperley & Long Mynd, north of Ludlow

evidence.excerpt: March 4th. We've come to Hamperley, north of Ludlow. Simon got feasts for everyone out of the freezer when we arrived.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Page A_0014_L opens with the tail of the Bath/Avebury trip ('the boys, Julius, Victorian statue replaced ... We visited Pouteney Bridge'); the break falls at 'March 4th. We've come to Hamperley, north of Ludlow.' Note the 'March 4th' date sits out of chronological order after the April 20-21 entries; the index places Hamperley/Long Mynd at p22 in 1990.

boundaries.title_guess: Dinas, Welshpool railway, Harlech & Barmouth with the Portals

evidence.excerpt: May 30th 1990. We have set off for Dinas with Matt Portal, Claire 7 & Peter 4. Jane is in Korea.
June 3rd. We pack up at Dinas and set off for home. ... In the afternoon we visit the highest waterfall in Wales, before reaching home.

boundaries.mixed_detail: Page A_0016_L opens with the tail of the Hamperley/Long Mynd trip ('John & Charles were too tired for another walk ... After tea we set off home'); the break falls at 'May 30th 1990. We have set off for Dinas with Matt Portal'.

boundaries.title_guess: Cherry Tree Farm, Woodford Halse & Canons Ashby (Daventry)

evidence.excerpt: June 23rd. Simon picked up Peter, Thomas & William from school & drove over to Long Buckby ... we arrived at Cherry Tree Farm campsite, Woodford Halse

boundaries.title_guess: Ardèche (France) & the Wegners in W. Germany

evidence.excerpt: July 25 1990. Simon, Eve, Thomas, William, Peter off to the Ardèche, France, & to visit the Wegners in W. Germany.
Once we were back in England we went to west of Folkestone to see the Channel Tunnel Exhibition Centre

open_at_end: Ardèche/Germany trip starting A_0022_L is still in progress at the end of the window: A_0030_R has the family just back in England visiting the Channel Tunnel Exhibition Centre (index p56 Calais, p57 Channel Tunnel Museum) before reaching home; the return home and trip end fall past A_0030.
notes: Window opens with front matter (A_0001) and the handwritten index spread (A_0002), both marked non_trip. Diary covers 1989 (pp1-15) and 1990 (pp16-56). Date order is mostly chronological except A_0014_L 'March 4th' (Hamperley) which appears after the April 20-21 Avebury/Bath entries - likely a March outing logged out of sequence; flagged with lower confidence. 'Malvern old boys' day' (A_0004_R), the Venetia stay (A_0010_L) and the Windmill day-out (A_0011_R) read as visits/day-outs rather than full van outings, marked non_trip with mixed-page breaks. spread_range reflects the spreads actually present (A_0001-A_0030); the book record nominally runs to A_0083.
